slots.notes: Top-level container comprising four cards in a 2x2 grid: an overview how the agency works. Each one has a heading (i.e. 'Discover', 'Design', 'Deliver', 'Optimize'), a body short description, and an accent-color icon (accent). Each card is treated as a separate section - four sections in a grid. The cards are all running text, so no data backend is needed. These are static descriptions - replace them with your brand's capabilities. Note: feature-cards.js - this is a different component than card-stats-reviewer.

Functional carousel chrome (not brand assets): previous/next arrows inside the two circular #D9D9D9 buttons (w-6 h-6 text-black, strokeWidth 1.5). Native source is lucide-react ArrowLeft / ArrowRight. Only swap if the brand has its own icon system; transparent so the grey button shows through.

Primary UI typeface for every visible string in this section: the section title, the large guillemet quote (font-light, tracking-tight, size 2rem), the name and title pair and card headings and text. Native placeholder is Google Sans. Supply the brand's preferred typeface.

mechanic: A carousel with a 2x2 grid of cards on desktop, 1x4 on mobile. Each card has an icon, a heading, and a description. There's a section header above the grid. Framer Motion useInView fary-InView with staggered delays (2s between cards). Left arrow, right arrow, and indicator dots are present as navigation elements. No data backend / swipe functionality. Static placeholder copy.
techDelivery: React 19 + TypeScript (Next.js) with tailwindcss/split-deviner styling (arbitrary values) and Framer Motion v12 + for carousel and entry animations. Icons from lucide-react.
reskinNotes: This is a carousel of four service-cards. It is a piece of static placeholder copy. The cards are static - there is no data backend or swipe functionality. The nav arrows are functional carousel chrome - copy them from lucide-react and turn them grey. The background is #f5f5f5 - it is not white. The accent color is recorded as inline: #3b82f6 (blue). Your nav arrows are functional - disabled but not hidden. These are static content cards.
